- [Type Erasers in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/type-erasers-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Explains two ways to build type erasers for protocols that cannot be used directly as standalone types. Use it to understand the mechanics and costs behind Any-style wrappers in older Swift code.
- [Tuples, Structs and Enums — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/tuples-structs-enums) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Compares tuples, structs, and enums as Swift data-modeling tools. Use it to clarify their structural differences before choosing a representation for a domain value.
- [A tiny networking library — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/tiny-networking-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Derives a small networking layer by composing focused Swift functions instead of adopting a full-featured library. Use it to study lightweight API wrapping and functional composition, while accounting for its older platform context.
- [SwiftUI Views are Lists — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-views-are-lists) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Argues that SwiftUI's View protocol can represent a list of views, not only a single rendered element. Use it to build a more accurate mental model for builders, conditionals, and container behavior.
- [SwiftUI View Model Ownership — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-view-model) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Investigates ownership and initialization of a view model created by a SwiftUI view, including the traps around init and state storage. Use it when deciding how a view should create and retain reference-model state.
- [Task Identity — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-task-identity) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Connects SwiftUI view identity and dependency tracking to the lifetime and restart behavior of tasks. Use it when asynchronous work appears to run with stale inputs or at unexpected times.
- [Lifetime of State Properties in SwiftUI — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-state-lifetime) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Explores how SwiftUI associates state-property storage with structural identity and view lifetime. Use it to reason about when state persists, resets, or moves as a view hierarchy changes.
- [SwiftUI Phase Animation "Bug" — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-phase-animation-bug) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Debugs unexpected phase-animation behavior that initially looks like a framework bug but has another cause. Use it as a focused diagnostic example before concluding that PhaseAnimator itself is broken.
- [Running Code When Your View Appears — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-on-appear-vs-task) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Compares onAppear and task as ways to start work when a SwiftUI view becomes active, emphasizing that their lifecycle semantics differ. Use it when choosing where asynchronous or expensive work should begin.
- [SwiftUI Environment Tips — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-environment-tips) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Examines SwiftUI environment design with attention to precise dependency tracking and avoiding unnecessary updates. Use it when passing shared styles or configuration through a view tree.
- [SwiftUI Binding Tips — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-binding-tricks) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Shows how dynamic member lookup and small transformations can make SwiftUI bindings easier to adapt. Use it when deriving focused bindings such as an inverted Boolean without repetitive closure code.
- [SwiftUI Alignment Guide Bug — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swiftui-alignment-guide-bug) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Documents an alignment-guide problem involving conditional SwiftUI content and demonstrates the unexpected layout result. Use it as a diagnostic lead, then verify whether the behavior remains in the target OS version.
- [Swift Tricks — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swift-tricks) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Collects small Swift utility patterns for tasks such as splitting collections and generating values lazily. Use it as an idea source, with extra scrutiny for language and standard-library changes since publication.
- [Swift Mirrors and JSON — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swift-mirrors-and-json) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Experiments with generating JSON-like dictionaries through Swift's early reflection facilities. Use it as historical exploration of Mirror-based serialization, not as a recommended replacement for Codable.
- [Some ideas for projects in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swift-ideas) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Lists project and library ideas that could be implemented or ported to Swift. Use it for learning-project inspiration rather than architectural guidance.
- [Swift and C functions — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swift-c-interop) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Demonstrates Swift and C interoperability by wrapping qsort and handling C function pointers. Use it to understand low-level bridging patterns, while checking modern compiler syntax and safety rules.
- [Swift Analytics — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/swift-analytics) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Compares functions, structs, enums, and protocols as ways to model analytics events in Swift. Use it to evaluate how much type structure an analytics layer actually needs.
- [Structs and mutation in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/structs-and-mutation-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Explains how mutation works for Swift structs and why mutating value types remains compatible with value semantics. Use it to sharpen the distinction between changing a value and sharing mutable identity.
- [Struct Semantics in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/struct-semantics-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Builds an efficient copy-on-write struct to illustrate Swift value semantics over shared storage. Use it when studying how standard-library-like value types can defer copies safely.
- [Sort Descriptors in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/sort-descriptors-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Replaces runtime-oriented Objective-C sort descriptors with composable Swift functions. Use it to study how higher-order functions can express ordering without dynamic key-path machinery.
- [You Don't Need Routes — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/simpler-swift-apis) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Questions whether an internal Swift API needs explicit route types and explores a smaller alternative for simple server operations. Use it when evaluating whether routing abstractions add useful guarantees or only ceremony.
- [Semantic Colors and Styles — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/semantic-colors) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Explores how SwiftUI semantic colors and styles resolve according to context such as appearance. Use it when designing adaptive styling that should remain meaningful across light, dark, and inherited environments.
- [Swift in Production: Scenery — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/scenery-launch) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A launch note for Scenery, described as the team's first fully Swift app, with product context around its release. Use it as historical evidence of early production Swift adoption, not current language guidance.
- [Repmin in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/repmin-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Implements the functional-programming repmin problem in Swift by combining tree traversals. Use it as an advanced exercise in recursive data transformations, not as everyday application architecture.
- [Rejected Swift Proposals — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/rejected-swift-proposals) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Reviews rejected Swift Evolution proposals to extract language-design lessons from their rationale. Use it for historical context on Swift's evolution and verify conclusions against the final accepted language behavior.
- [Struct References — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/references) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Experiments with using Swift key paths to create reference-like access into value-typed structures. Use it as a research prototype; the author does not present it as production-proven design.
- [QuickCheck in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/quickcheck-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Ports ideas from Haskell's QuickCheck into an early Swift experiment with property-based testing. Use it to understand the testing model, while expecting substantial syntax and library drift.
- [Protocols in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/protocols-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Walks through making a custom singly linked list conform to Swift's Collection protocol. Use it to study protocol requirements and collection design, with version-aware checks for modern associated types and indices.
- [Protocol Oriented Programming is Not a Silver Bullet — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/protocol-oriented-programming) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Argues that protocol-oriented programming is often overapplied and that simpler concrete designs may solve the problem better. Use it as a counterweight when evaluating whether a protocol adds a real abstraction boundary.
- [Phase Animator Behavior — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/phase-animator) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Investigates PhaseAnimator behavior through experiments aimed at explaining unexpected results. Use it to build a deeper SwiftUI animation model and verify details against the current SDK.
- [Monads in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/monads-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Introduces monads through compact Swift examples in an attempt to make the functional-programming concept less mysterious. It is useful for conceptual orientation, with language syntax and idioms that may now be dated.
- [A Micro Auto Layout DSL — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/micro-autolayout-dsl) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Builds a very small Auto Layout helper layer to reduce repetitive constraint code without adopting a large dependency. Use it as an example of a deliberately narrow internal DSL and reassess it against current layout APIs.
- [When Matched Geometry Effect Doesn't Work — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/matched-geometry-effect) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Investigates a SwiftUI matched-geometry transition that behaves unexpectedly and traces the result to declaration order. It is useful when building a mental model for matchedGeometryEffect and diagnosing apparently incorrect animations.
- [Literate Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/literate-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Describes a literate-programming tool that combines Swift code with Markdown so book examples can be executed and maintained alongside prose. It is useful for documentation and technical-publishing workflows, especially where language changes can invalidate examples.
- [Lenses in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/lenses-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Explores lenses as composable getter-and-setter pairs for reading and updating nested data in Swift. Use it to understand a functional approach to immutable-style updates rather than as a required Swift design pattern.
- [Parsing JSON in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/json-parsing-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Explores a composable, function-oriented approach to decoding JSON in early Swift. Use it for historical parser-design ideas and compare them with current Codable and contemporary parsing libraries.
- [Functional Programming in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/functional-programming-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Announces the Functional Programming in Swift book and explains the motivation for applying functional ideas to Apple's then-new language. It provides historical orientation rather than the book's technical content.
- [FormatStyle Guide — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/format-style-guide) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Introduces an interactive browser-based guide to Swift Foundation FormatStyle APIs, implemented with WebAssembly. Use the linked guide for quickly comparing formatting capabilities and verify availability against current Foundation documentation.
- [Dynamic Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/dynamic-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Examines concerns about Swift's early lack of dynamic-language features and considers ways the ecosystem might recover useful expressiveness. It is valuable as historical language-design discussion, not a description of current Swift capabilities.
- [Deep Understanding while using LLMs — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/deep-understanding-while-using-llms) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Argues that using an LLM can accelerate delivery while also bypassing the struggle needed to build a durable mental model. Use it when designing learning or workshop workflows where understanding matters as much as output.
- [Classes That Conform To Protocols — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/classes-and-protocols) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Shows type-erasure-based workarounds for storing a class-constrained value that must also conform to a protocol in early Swift. Use it to study the underlying type-system problem and verify whether modern Swift offers simpler syntax.
- [Building Pancake — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/building-pancake) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A case study of collaborating on a SwiftUI Mac app within a one-month constraint. Use it for product-scoping and development-workflow ideas, while validating any technical choices against the finished code and current APIs.
- [The Boring Choice — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/boring-choice) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Argues from experience that mature, conventional technology can be a better production choice than an exciting experiment. Use it to frame reliability and maintenance trade-offs, not as a blanket preference for old tools.
- [The Blub Paradox in Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/blub-paradox-in-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Uses the Blub paradox to discuss why unfamiliar Swift language features can initially look unnecessary or excessive. It is useful for language-learning and API-design perspective, with examples interpreted in their historical Swift context.
- [Bindings — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/binding-with-get-set) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Compares key-path-derived SwiftUI bindings with manually constructed Binding(get:set:) values and explains behavioral differences that affect view updates and animation. Use it when diagnosing custom-binding surprises rather than assuming both forms are interchangeable.
- [Agentic Coding — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/agentic-coding) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Reports experiments using coding agents for SwiftUI work, including productive feedback loops, surprising failures, and tasks that still require a strong mental model. Use it to shape an agent-assisted workflow without treating the observations as universal benchmarks.
- [Advanced Swift — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/advanced-swift) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** Introduces Advanced Swift's focus on wrapping C libraries for Swift, using cmark and libuv to illustrate pointer, buffer, and ownership conversions. Use it as historical context for Swift/C interoperability design and the memory-management boundary such wrappers must expose.
- [Weeknotes № 47 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2024-47) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A personal account of delivering an on-site SwiftUI workshop in London, with emphasis on face-to-face teaching and visual explanations. Useful for understanding the authors' workshop approach rather than SwiftUI APIs themselves.
- [Weeknotes № 45 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2024-45) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A practical look at preparing customized SwiftUI workshops, from selecting exercises to building a tailored starter repository. Useful for designing client-specific technical training.
- [Weeknotes № 40 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2024-39) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A conference weeknote about speaking at Swift Connection in Paris and presenting techniques for building a technical talk. Useful for a practitioner's perspective on conference preparation and delivery.
- [Weeknotes № 23-24 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2024-24) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A mixed personal and professional weeknote covering a distributed workshop, teaching HTML at a local hackerspace, Swift Talk recording, and travel to California. Useful as background on the author's teaching and community work.
- [Weeknotes № 12-13 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2024-13) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A technical project note about implementing shapes and gradients for the SwiftUI Field Guide and discovering complexity in Canvas-based rendering. Useful for understanding the exploratory work behind the guide.
- [Weeknotes № 5-7 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2024-07) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A launch weeknote for the SwiftUI Field Guide, describing the concentrated effort required to make the project public. Useful as origin and release context for the guide.
- [Weeknotes № 4 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2024-04) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A technical progress note on adding transitions and frame animation to the web-based SwiftUI visualization project. Useful for understanding the capabilities and current limits of its custom rendering framework.
- [Weeknotes № 51-52 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-52) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A year-end project note on implementing proposed designs for a SwiftUI layout-explanation website in both SwiftUI and a web port. Useful for following the early development of the Field Guide concept.
- [Weeknotes № 49-50 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-50) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A development weeknote about recording material on tweakable values, redesigning objc.io properties, and refining a SwiftUI layout-explanation site. Useful as background on several related educational-tool experiments.
- [Weeknotes № 43 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-43) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A project-origin note arguing that SwiftUI layout deserves clearer, interactive explanations and describing an experiment toward a public website. Useful for understanding the motivation behind the later SwiftUI Field Guide.
- [Weeknotes № 39 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-39) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A release weeknote for Thinking in SwiftUI that also notes ongoing format considerations and programming experiments. Useful for understanding the book's publication state and adjacent work.
- [Weeknotes № 38 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-38) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A technical weeknote about building an interactive marquee component and adapting its SwiftUI implementation beyond a conventional passive view. Useful as context for experiments with gestures and animation behavior.
- [Weeknotes № 37 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-37) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A project update on finalizing Thinking in SwiftUI for print while developing a typing-recorder app and exploring UIViewRepresentable behavior. Useful for following several active experiments rather than one finished technique.
- [Weeknotes № 35 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-35) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A technical progress note on reproducing SwiftUI keyframe behavior, including a compact Catmull-Rom implementation tested in an older macOS app. Useful as context for animation compatibility experiments.
- [Weeknotes № 34 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-34) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A development note on refining a custom SwiftUI keyframe implementation and trying to match the framework's Catmull-Rom interpolation. Useful for understanding the iterative comparison behind the experiment.
- [Weeknotes № 29-32 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-32) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A mixed holiday and project update that describes a backward-compatible reimplementation of SwiftUI-style keyframes and preparation for conference presentations. Useful as context for the animation experiment's goals and trade-offs.
- [Weeknotes № 27 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-27) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A release-preparation note covering the substantial iOS 17 additions to Thinking in SwiftUI's animation chapter, including phase and keyframe animations. Useful for understanding the book update's scope.
- [Weeknotes № 26 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-26) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A book-development reflection on revising the SwiftUI layout chapter and investing heavily in clearer explanations. Useful as editorial context for readers interested in the authors' model of layout behavior.
- [Weeknotes № 22 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-22) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A technical project note on extending an attributed-string builder so embedded content can receive SwiftUI environment modifiers. Useful as context for composing richer attributed content.
- [Weeknotes № 21 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-21) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A development weeknote about recreating Git's core read-only data structures in Swift and then experimenting with writing them. Useful as an approachable pointer to Git's underlying object model.
- [Weeknotes № 19 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-19) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A production update on redrawing Thinking in SwiftUI diagrams and revising explanations as the authors' understanding deepened. Useful for seeing how technical models and visual communication co-evolved.
- [Weeknotes № 16 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-16) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A technical tooling update on splitting a private SwiftUI tree-diagram library into separate parsing and drawing packages. Useful for thinking about boundaries in reusable visualization infrastructure.
- [Weeknotes № 12 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-12) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A mixed personal and work update covering house projects, Swift Talk recording, and continued writing. Useful mainly as background on the author's concurrent commitments.
- [Weeknotes № 10 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-10) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A production note on sending revised material about SwiftUI geometry, view-tree structure, and environment behavior through technical review. Useful for tracking the book's review process and conceptual scope.
- [Weeknotes № 8 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-08) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A book-development reflection on rewriting every Thinking in SwiftUI chapter and adding a view-tree chapter after deeper research and workshop experience. Useful for understanding why the new edition changed substantially.
- [Weeknotes № 2 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-02) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** A technical project note on recording the construction of a result-builder-based attributed-string library that combines Markdown, plain text, and embedded content. Useful as an entry point to that library's design.
- [Weeknotes № 1 — Chris Eidhof](https://chris.eidhof.nl/post/2023-01) — Chris Eidhof · article catalogue
  **NeKI brief:** The opening weeknote in a new weekly-log format, centered on rewriting Thinking in SwiftUI. Useful as a starting point for following the 2023 book-development series.

## Newsletter and related leads

- [ImplementedSE-0525Safe loading API for `RawSpan`](https://github.com/apple/swift-evolution/blob/main/proposals/0525-rawspan-safe-loading-api.md) — SwiftLee Weekly · Issue 323 · 2026-07-21T14:05:55.000Z — Source repository · Topics: Swift · Architecture
  **NeKI brief:** Provides the linked Swift or Apple-platform source repository for ImplementedSE-0525Safe loading API for `RawSpan`. Use it to inspect proposal details, implementation code, or release changes directly, while checking compatibility and maintenance status before adopting it.
- [The Anatomy of a Reusable SwiftUI View](https://alexanderweiss.dev/blog/2026-07-12-the-anatomy-of-a-reusable-swiftui-view) — iOS Dev Weekly · Issue 759 · 17th July 2026 — Article · Topics: Swift · SwiftUI · Observation & State Management
  **NeKI brief:** Examines how SwiftUI view responsibilities, inputs, and composition boundaries affect reuse. Useful when extracting components that remain readable, previewable, and stable under changing state.
- [Rendering SwiftUI Previews with Xcode’s MCP Server](https://cuteios.dev/2026/07/14/previews-and-mcp) — iOS Dev Weekly · Issue 759 · 17th July 2026 — Article · Topics: Xcode · AI Development · Swift
  **NeKI brief:** Builds a SwiftUI preview gallery by combining Xcode’s MCP server, project context, and generated preview metadata. The article maps the moving parts and current limitations, making it useful when evaluating agent-assisted preview tooling.
- [MistKit](https://github.com/brightdigit/MistKit) — iOS Dev Tools · iOS Dev Tools: MistKit, Swift Scribe, cmux - iOS Dev Tools · 2026-07-16T16:45:11.086Z — Source repository · Topics: Swift · Swift Package Manager
  **NeKI brief:** MistKit packages CloudKit Web Services access for server-side Swift and command-line tools, separating backend automation from Apple's client frameworks. Useful when a service needs CloudKit data without running inside an iOS app.
- [Swift Scribe](https://github.com/FluidInference/swift-scribe) — iOS Dev Tools · iOS Dev Tools: MistKit, Swift Scribe, cmux - iOS Dev Tools · 2026-07-16T16:45:11.086Z — Source repository · Topics: Swift · AI Development
  **NeKI brief:** Swift Scribe combines local microphone transcription with on-device summarization on current Apple OS releases, without external dependencies. Useful for evaluating a privacy-preserving speech pipeline and its deployment-version constraints.
- [WWDC 2026 Developer Tools: Xcode 27, Swift, Foundation Models](https://andrew.ooo/answers/wwdc-2026-developer-tools-xcode-swift-foundation-models-june-2026) — Those Who Swift · Issue 275 · 2026-07-15 — Article · Topics: Swift · Xcode · AI Development
  **NeKI brief:** Surveys the WWDC 2026 developer-tool changes across Xcode, Swift, and Foundation Models. Use it as a release-oriented map of new workflows, then verify specific APIs, deployment requirements, and availability in Apple's current documentation.
- [Swift Bits: My Top Xcode CI Environment Variables](https://antongubarenko.substack.com/p/swift-bits-my-top-xcode-ci-environment) — Those Who Swift · Issue 275 · 2026-07-15 — Article · Topics: Xcode · Swift
  **NeKI brief:** Collects Xcode-related environment variables that can make CI jobs more predictable and diagnosable. Follow it when hardening build scripts, while checking each variable against the project's Xcode version and the behavior of its hosted runners.
- [Splitting Large SwiftUI Views in the Apple's way](https://emredegirmenci.substack.com/p/splitting-large-swiftui-views-in) — SwiftLee Weekly · Issue 322 · 2026-07-14T14:06:22.000Z — Article · Topics: Swift · SwiftUI · Xcode
  **NeKI brief:** Explains Apple's own decomposition techniques for splitting large SwiftUI views, including state and builder boundaries. Useful for reducing oversized bodies while keeping data flow explicit, previewable, and understandable to teammates.
- [anyAppleOS in Swift 6.4](https://livsycode.com/swift/swift-6-4-adds-anyappleos-for-cleaner-availability-checks) — SwiftLee Weekly · Issue 322 · 2026-07-14T14:06:22.000Z — Article · Topics: Swift
  **NeKI brief:** Explains Swift 6.4's anyAppleOS type as a way to express availability checks without spelling every Apple platform. Use it when auditing cross-platform branches, while confirming the exact compiler and SDK version before adoption.
- [Custom bindings in SwiftUI: closures vs subscripts](https://nilcoalescing.com/blog/CustomBindingsInSwiftUIClosuresVsSubscripts?ref=createwithswift.com) — Create with Swift · Issue 115 · 2026-07-10T15:00:44.000Z — Article · Topics: Observation & State Management · Swift · SwiftUI
  **NeKI brief:** Compares closure-based and subscript-based custom Bindings in SwiftUI, including how each expresses read and write access. Use it when designing reusable bindings and choosing an approach that keeps transformations clear, composable, and maintainable.
- [SwiftUI Performance](https://livsycode.com/swiftui/the-swiftui-performance-skill) — iOS Dev Weekly · Issue 758 · 10th July 2026 — Article · Topics: Swift · SwiftUI · Performance
  **NeKI brief:** Introduces an agent skill for investigating SwiftUI performance through view lifecycle, rendering behavior, and measurement-oriented workflows. Follow it when giving coding agents repeatable performance diagnostics, while validating conclusions with Instruments and real app traces.
